Show / Hide Table of Contents

Class TenantDbContextFactory<TContext>

Creates TContext instances configured for a specific tenant. Used by the DB-per-tenant strategy to swap connection strings at runtime.

Inheritance
object
TenantDbContextFactory<TContext>

Namespace: AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore
Assembly: AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EFCore.dll
Syntax
public class TenantDbContextFactory<TContext> where TContext : DbContext
Type Parameters
Name Description
TContext

Your application's DbContext type.

Constructors

| Edit this page View Source

TenantDbContextFactory(ITenantContext, IServiceProvider, Action<DbContextOptionsBuilder, string>)

Declaration
public TenantDbContextFactory(ITenantContext tenantContext, IServiceProvider serviceProvider, Action<DbContextOptionsBuilder, string> configureOptions)
Parameters
Type Name Description
ITenantContext tenantContext

The active tenant context.
IServiceProvider serviceProvider

The DI service provider.
Action<DbContextOptionsBuilder, string> configureOptions

A delegate that receives the options builder and the tenant's connection string, and configures the database provider. Example:

(opts, cs) => opts.UseSqlServer(cs)

Methods

| Edit this page View Source

CreateContext()

Creates a TContext using the current tenant's connection string.

Declaration
public TContext CreateContext()
Returns
Type Description
TContext
Exceptions
Type Condition
InvalidOperationException

Thrown if the current tenant has no ConnectionString.
